//notes: modelled after size Large, because that's the size the Haley MOLLE placard is sold in
description: A MOLLE placard with a pair of buckles.  Can attach to a harness to form a wearable chest rig.  Alternatively, it could be attached to plate carrier to serve as space for more pouches.

variants.description: A MOLLE placard with a pair of buckles.  Can be combined with a harness to make a compact, minimal chest rig.  It can also be worn on some plate carriers via the QASM interface, as a replaceable alternative to a predefined PALS webbing or a set of pockets.  <variant:multicam>

description: A compact chest rig chassis with a pair of buckles.  Features four rifle magazine pouches, four pistol magazine pouches, and a general pouch for small supplies.  Can attach to a harness to form a wearable chest rig.  Alternatively, it could be attached to a plate carrier to serve as a set of extra pouches.

It can also be worn on plate carriers via the QASM interface, as a replaceable alternative to a predefined PALS webbing or a set of pockets.  <variant:multicam>

//weight: we assume the harness that ships with the chassis is the Haley Strategic X-harness (or a very similar alternative), for the purposes of calculating weight
we presume, based on weights of other rigs and LBVs, that 18.3 oz/520 g is the weight of the entire assembly, including the harness
18.3 oz - 8.4 oz (weight of the harness) = 9.9 oz = 280.66 g

notes: length and height are from the PALS webbing
width (depth) is presumed based on overall depth of the rig

pocket_data.//max_item_length: 6 in., the length of the pouch

//price: pure guess based on how other modular options have been priced when sold separately

//notes: this placard comes with hard MP2 magazine-retainer inserts by default
description: A placard with a pair of buckles.  Holds three individual rifle magazines.  Can attach to a harness to form a wearable chest rig.  Alternatively, it could be attached to a plate carrier to serve as magazine carry.

variants.description: A placard with three rifle-magazine pouches, augmented by a pair of buckles.  Can be combined with a harness to make a compact, minimal chest rig.  It can also be worn on some plate carriers via the QASM interface, as a replaceable alternative sewn-in magazine pockets.  <variant:multicam>

//notes: the webbing underneath the magazines (advertised in the store description) is accounted for
description: A chest rig chassis with PALS webbing and several rifle-magazine pouches, supported by a pair of buckles.  Each side also has two flat pockets for intelligence items, such as maps or documents.  Can attach to a harness to form a wearable chest rig.  Alternatively, it could be attached to a plate carrier to serve as a combination of magazine carry and additional space for pouches.

variants.description: A slim chassis, split into two by a zipper.  Four rifle-magazine pockets can fit a pair each.  Pockets inside each side could be used to store flat objects like maps, notes, and IDs.  The chassis also features plenty of PALS webbing.   <variant:multicam>

A slim chassis, split into two by a zipper.  Four rifle-magazine pockets can fit a pair each.  Pockets inside each side could be used to store flat objects like maps, notes, and IDs.  The chassis also features plenty of PALS webbing.   <variant:coyote>

A slim chassis, split into two by a zipper.  Four rifle-magazine pockets can fit a pair each.  Pockets inside each side could be used to store flat objects like maps, notes, and IDs.  The chassis also features plenty of PALS webbing.   <variant:wildwood>.

we pretend it does to maintain parity with the H-harness from the same manufacturer
variants.description: A slim chassis, split into two by a zipper.  Four rifle-magazine pockets can fit a pair each.  Pockets inside each side could be used to store flat objects like maps, notes, and IDs.  The chassis also features plenty of PALS webbing.   <variant:woodland>.

//: special variant for the Prepped Patriot profession
this cannot spawn in the wild
variants.description: A slim chassis, split into two by a zipper.  Four rifle-magazine pockets can fit a pair each.  Pockets inside each side could be used to store flat objects like maps, notes, and IDs.  The chassis also features plenty of PALS webbing.  This one features a custom paint job, turning the rig into a representation of all things American: the flag, a bald eagle, fireworks, and loose rounds of .50 BMG.

//weight: 1.17 lbs (weight of the rig as a whole) - 200 g (weight of the H-harness we use for this chassis) = 330.7 g

notes: height assumed from width of an average STANAG magazine + a little extra for the pouch's textile

//notes: side pockets are copied from the MAV 2 Piece rig

notes: said to span the entire size of the panel

//max_item_volume: based on the calculation of volume for a map of New York City subway
we exaggerate the height (thickness) of the reference map in order to allow thicker paper items to fit

//uses: the original reference had the lower limit of the 20-round magazine, length-wise
//min_item_length: this is necessary to allow `ruger_arr_mag` (100 mm) to spawn
without this, the game throws an error when trying to spawn ghost(?) magazines on load

//notes: we model the placard as having magazine inserts, because we don't currently include inserts independently
rifle-mag inserts come with an MP2 insert each
weight reference for the panel itself
harness weight does not appear to be available online (not sold by Condor, model unknown)
description: A compact chest rig chassis with two rifle-magazine pouches, one pistol-magazine pouch, a medium-sized general pouch, and some PALS webbing for additional pouches.  Limited in capacity, but unencumbering.

variants.description: A compact chassis meant to form an unencumbering, if not capacity-heavy, chest rig.  The general pouch at the front is coupled with an insert pocket at the back and external PALS webbing.  The insert pocket is filled with three inserts: two for a rifle magazine each, and one for a pistol magazine or a utility item, such as a multitool.   <variant:multicam>

A compact chassis meant to form an unencumbering, if not capacity-heavy, chest rig.  The general pouch at the front is coupled with an insert pocket at the back and external PALS webbing.  The insert pocket is filled with three inserts: two for a rifle magazine each, and one for a pistol magazine or a utility item, such as a multitool.  <variant:coyote>

A compact chassis meant to form an unencumbering, if not capacity-heavy, chest rig.  The general pouch at the front is coupled with an insert pocket at the back and external PALS webbing.  The insert pocket is filled with three inserts: two for a rifle magazine each, and one for a pistol magazine or a utility item, such as a multitool.  <variant:olive>

A compact chassis meant to form an unencumbering, if not capacity-heavy, chest rig.  The general pouch at the front is coupled with an insert pocket at the back and external PALS webbing.  The insert pocket is filled with three inserts: two for a rifle magazine each, and one for a pistol magazine or a utility item, such as a multitool.  <variant:black>

//weight: + 1 oz = 28.35 g × 2 = 56.7 g (2 × rifle mag insert)
//price: as the harness is not sold separately, we can only guess at its price
we assume a fairly low price relative to the X-harness we model in the mod due to Condor being known for making lower-price products

//notes: this item does not allow the bib to be adjusted (tucked in or set up, the latter being the in-game configuration)
bib adjustment shouldn't matter too much anyway because the encumbrance effect would be almost non-existent by the game's current standards
it also doesn't presently allow for a ballistic plate to be inserted, partly for balance reasons (to be reviewed)
we are modelling the front pouches as if they are irremovable, which seems to be the case, with reviewers not saying a word about it
reference chart is explicit about this model having double-magazine pouches
description: A large, comfortable chest rig chassis with plenty of PALS webbing.  It has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as with two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra webbing.

variants.description: A large chassis with plenty of PALS webbing.  Much of the rig is padded on the inside, making wearing it more comfortable.  The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  <variant:multicam>

A large chassis with plenty of PALS webbing.  Much of the rig is padded on the inside, making wearing it more comfortable.  The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  <variant:coyote>

The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  <variant:olive>

The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  <variant:black>

A large chassis with plenty of PALS webbing.  Much of the rig is padded on the inside, making wearing it more comfortable.  The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  <variant:fde>

A large chassis with plenty of PALS webbing.  Much of the rig is padded on the inside, making wearing it more comfortable.  The rig has four rifle magazine pouches, each fitting up to two STANAG magazines, as well as two spacious side pouches and a bib that offers some extra storage and webbing.  This one is colored stone grey, with slight greenish tint.

//weight: 39 oz = 1105.6 g (weight of the complete rig) - 200 g (weight of the H-harness)

notes: width is 8 cells of PALS webbing (8 × 1.5 in. = 12 in.) + 0.5 in. extra, from visual reference
height is 5 rows of PALS webbing (5 webbing rows + 4 spacers = 9 × 1 in. = 9 in.)
length (depth) is presumed thickness of all the material, with pouches collapsed when empty
trim assumes all the material evens out once the pouches are folded onto themselves
dimensions acquired from MOLLE cell count
trim is for slanted pouch MOLLE area
width is 6 cells of PALS webbing (6 × 1.5 in. = 9 in.) + 0.25 in. extra, from visual reference
height is 7 rows of PALS webbing (3 webbing rows + 4 spacers = 7 × 1 in. = 7 in.)
length (depth) is presumed thickness of all the material, with pouches collapsed when empty
trim assumes all the material evens out once the pouches are folded onto themselves

//coverage: 5% × 2 from side pouches covering the same area as SAPI side plates

notes: dimensions acquired from MOLLE cell count
trim is for slanted pouch MOLLE area

pocket_data.//max_item_length: 9 in., so that the items inside could be comfortably taken out

//price: 219 USD (price of the complete rig) - 48 USD (price of the H-harness)
